channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: El futuro del seguro se juega en la gestión del talento
item.description: El sector asegurador afronta uno de los mayores desafíos de su historia en materia de gestión del talento, impulsado por la automatización, el riesgo climático y el cambio en las expectativas de la fuerza laboral. Así lo señala un reciente informe de Aon , que sitúa la construcción de una estrategia de talento preparada para el futuro como una prioridad crítica para las aseguradoras . El rol incluye la gestión de proveedores, supervisión de mantenimientos y apoyo logístico interno para garantizar un entorno de trabajo eficiente y seguro.

item.title: Del nuevo registro de VPL al deber de información: el sector asegurador se adapta a los cambios legales
item.description: También destacó el nuevo registro de los VPL según el RD 52/2026. A continuación, José Luis Quilis, responsable de suscripción y siniestros de CGPA , trajo a colación el deber de información de los Mediadores de Seguros subrayando los principios generales de honestidad, imparcialidad y equidad, dando publicidad precisa, clara y no engañosa y evitar conflicto te intereses a la hora de tomar decisiones vía remuneración, que se recogen en el Real Decreto-ley 3/2020 , que “ha supuesto un punto de inflexión en el deber de asesoramiento de los distribuidores de seguros”, concluyó Quilis. Roberto Hernández, director de siniestros de Zurich y Francisco Lisbona, director de Zurich Resilience Solutions , expusieron los retos para el sector asegurador tras la DANA .

item.title: Siete aseguradoras se suman a ‘La Hora del Planeta’
item.description: El sector asegurador se ha unido un año más a ‘La Hora del Planeta’ , la iniciativa global impulsada por WWF que busca concienciar sobre el cambio climático y fomentar el ahorro y uso eficiente de energía. La iniciativa recuerda problemas ambientales como el calentamiento global y los fenómenos extremos que afectan cada vez con más intensidad a todo el planeta. La acción tuvo lugar el sábado, entre las 20.30 y las 21.30 horas, con el apagado simbólico de luces en oficinas y edificios emblemáticos.

item.title: LNG Supply Cut Further After Cyclone Hits Australian Plants
item.description: Three Australian plants that provide about 8% of the world’s liquefied natural gas have had their output curbed by a cyclone, in a further blow to mainly Asian buyers reeling from the halt of shipments from Qatar. “Temporary shut-ins at Australian LNG plants come at the worst time for LNG buyers looking to replace supply from Qatar,” said Josh Runciman, lead analyst for Australian gas at the Institute for Energy Economics and Financial Analysis. North West Shelf’s offshore workforce was evacuated in line with Woodside’s cyclone preparation arrangements and production is expected to recommence after they return, according to the company’s spokesperson.

item.title: HLDI: Safety Benefits Stack up From Driver Assistance Features
item.description: Safety benefits from these driver assistance features also increase as systems get bundled and updated, according to a study from the Highway Loss Data Institute examining the impact of advanced driver assistance systems on Mazda models from 2015 to 2023. The HLDI evaluated several other features which include blind spot detection with rear cross traffic alert, curve-adaptive headlights, heads-up display and traffic sign recognition. Smaller reductions in safety benefits were associated with curve-adaptive headlights and the heads-up display.

item.title: Delta Insurance New Zealand promotes Dinesh Murali to managing director
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Lloyd’s seeks a quiet sunset, but storm clouds remain
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: South Korea non-life carriers’ insurance income plunges 32% despite premium growth
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: AM Best upgrades Ocean Re’s credit ratings
item.description: Ocean International Reinsurance Company Limited (Ocean Re) has had its Financial Strength Rating upgraded to A (Excellent) from A- (Excellent) and its Long-Term Issuer Credit Rating upgraded to “a” (Excellent) from “a-” (Excellent) by AM Best, with the outlook of these ratings revised to stable from positive. AM Best notes that the upgrade reflects Ocean Re’s “very strong” balance sheet strength and operating performance, neutral business profile, and appropriate enterprise risk management. Ocean Re’s business development strategy clearly identifies an increase in the proportion of traditional reinsurance in its portfolio, as compared with its captive portfolio, explained AM Best.

item.title: Arch Insurance UK Regional Division name Tinworth Senior P&C Underwriter
item.description: Henry Tinworth has joined Arch Insurance UK Regional Division as Senior Property & Casualty Underwriter, effective immediately, based in Leeds. His wealth of trading and technical expertise, combined with his service-led approach, will be invaluable as we deepen our broker partnerships, deliver tailored solutions for complex client risks, and strengthen our presence across the region.” Tinworth is joining Arch from DUAL where he was most recently a Senior Underwriter. In his new role, Tinworth will leverage his 10 years of UK insurance marketexperience to drive new business development across the property and casualty portfolio with a regional focus on the north.

item.title: Converge gets additional cyber insurance capacity from Lloyd’s
item.description: Converge, a company specialising in cyber risk management and underwriting, has secured additional underwriting capacity from Lloyd’s, the world’s oldest insurance and reinsurance marketplace, for companies with annual revenues up to $5 billion. The expanded capacity from both Lloyd’s and Obsidian reflects strong market confidence in Converge’s strategy of combining rigorous underwriting discipline with advanced technology to achieve better risk selection and pricing precision. The expanded capacity will be available across Converge’s full suite of products, allowing brokers to access comprehensive cyber solutions.”

item.title: Lines Persist at Some Airports After Trump Signs TSA Pay Memo
item.description: Several US airports advised travelers on Saturday to arrive at least four hours before their flights because of long security lines even after President Donald Trump said Transportation Security Administration workers would be paid by tapping funds from his 2025 tax and spending bill. Trump signed a memo Friday directing TSA personnel to be paid as he tried to alleviate disruptions at US airports, but it remains unclear how much it will do to improve wait times at security checkpoints, which have varied widely at different airports. Trump’s memo directed DHS and the White House budget office to use funds that have a “reasonable and logical nexus” to agency operations to provide employees who have worked without pay “with the compensation and benefits that would have accrued to them if not for” the shutdown.

item.title: Bank of America Agrees to Pay $72.5M to Settle Epstein Accusers’ Lawsuit
item.description: Bank of America agreed to pay $72.5 million to settle a civil lawsuit brought by women who accused the bank of facilitating their sexual abuse by Jeffrey Epstein, court records showed on Friday. Bank of America has said Doe alleged merely that it provided routine services to people who at the time had no known links to Epstein, and that any suggestion that it was more deeply involved was “threadbare and meritless.” Rakoff ruled in January that Bank of America must face Doe’s claims that it knowingly benefited from Epstein’s sex trafficking and obstructed enforcement of the federal Trafficking Victims Protection Act. Doe’s lawyers have also sued other alleged enablers of Epstein’s sex trafficking, and in 2023 reached settlements of $290 million with JPMorgan Chase and $75 million with Deutsche Bank on behalf of his accusers.

item.title: Trump Unveils New Loan Guarantees for Beleaguered Farmers
item.description: President Donald Trump announced new loan guarantees for farmers and food suppliers, a bid to bolster US agricultural communities squeezed by the impact of the Iran war, tariffs and other challenges. Trump said the loan guarantees would cover farmers and food suppliers, including vegetable, grain and seed farmers, cattle, pig and poultry producers, as well as grocery wholesalers and others. Trump did not specify a dollar amount for the guarantees.

item.title: Crawford Contractor Connection partners with Helixco
item.description: Contractor Connection, a Crawford & Company business that operates a managed repair network, partnered with Helixco , a technology platform designed to capture and validate time and materials documentation in real time across complex commercial repair projects. Contractor Connection will leverage Helixco’s platform to provide real-time visibility into labor, equipment and cost documentation throughout the life of a commercial claim. By integrating Helixco’s platform into our commercial program, we are strengthening consistency, accountability and cost confidence at scale for our carrier partners while supporting efficient execution across our contractor network.” – Lance Malcolm, president, U.S.

item.title: Alacrity launches AI-driven claims platform
item.description: Alacrity Solutions is an independent provider of insurance claims management services in North America. The post Alacrity launches AI-driven claims platform appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights .

item.title: AXA Italia returns to TV with savings and investment-focused campaign
item.description: The storyline follows a child imagining future ambitions, reinforcing the message that building financial security starts with everyday decisions. The post AXA Italia returns to TV with savings and investment-focused campaign appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights .

item.title: Insurance Apostles files trademark for agricultural insurance agency brand
item.description: The positioning suggests a niche agency model targeting the agricultural segment. The post Insurance Apostles files trademark for agricultural insurance agency brand appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights .

item.title: China Life Insurance posts strong profit growth driven by investment performance and product innovation
item.description: China Life Insurance has reported a significant rise in profitability for 2025, supported by robust investment returns and continued progress in shifting toward higher-value insurance products. The post China Life Insurance posts strong profit growth driven by investment performance and product innovation appeared first on Insurtech Insights .

item.title: Corebridge Financial and Equitable Holdings agree $22bn merger to create $1.5tn platform
item.description: Corebridge Financial and Equitable Holdings have announced a landmark $22 billion all-stock merger, forming one of the largest retirement, life insurance, and asset management platforms in the United States. The post Corebridge Financial and Equitable Holdings agree $22bn merger to create $1.5tn platform appeared first on Insurtech Insights .

item.title: Crawford & Company names W. Bruce Swain Jr. President & CEO
item.description: The company also announced that Jesse Crawford, Sr. will transition to an honorary board role. The post Crawford & Company names W.

item.title: Marsh McLennan Agency launches healthcare pricing tool
item.description: The tool uses data from Transparency in Coverage regulations to provide provider-level cost insights. The post Marsh McLennan Agency launches healthcare pricing tool appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights .

item.title: Clearcover taps Strada to automate customer operations
item.description: Clearcover plans to expand the partnership into claims-related workflows and additional servicing use cases in the coming months. The post Clearcover taps Strada to automate customer operations appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights .

item.title: Berkshire bets the Buffett way; Mosaic’s exit casts a shadow
item.description: This week's newsletter discusses an IAN exclusive, Peak Re's leadership change,Allianz and Jio's JV, 1.4 renewals, Berkshire Hathaway's Japan foray and Mosaic's Singapore exit. The post Berkshire bets the Buffett way; Mosaic’s exit casts a shadow appeared first on InsuranceAsia News .

item.title: Krishnamoorthy Rao returns to Generali to lead India P&C JV
item.description: Rao was formerly with Generali in India and Malaysia for over 16 years. The post Krishnamoorthy Rao returns to Generali to lead India P&C JV appeared first on InsuranceAsia News .

item.title: HawkSoft Integrates RevitPay for Agency Payment Processing
item.description: The agency management system now supports direct API payments to accelerate premium collection, automate reconciliation and improve cash flow visibility.

item.title: Generali Hong Kong Deploys CoverGo AI for Claims Processing
item.description: The insurer has implemented an AI document processing agent to automate health claims, reduce manual work and improve accuracy.

item.title: Crypto ‘Insurance’ Might Not Protect You From Theft
item.description: When Matthew Allan realized nearly $100,000 in Bitcoin was missing from his Coinbase account, he wasn’t too worried. He had signed up for Coinbase One, a $29.99 monthly subscription that promised up to $1 million of account protection.

item.title: Bessent Says Hormuz Ships Insurance Program to Start Soon
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said a U.S. insurance program meant to boost shipping through the Strait of Hormuz will begin soon, a move that may help revive flows of much of the world’s oil and gas supplies. Bessent’s predictions, delivered …

item.title: Toyota’s China JVs Recall 560,000 SUVs Over Seat Safety Risk
item.description: Toyota Motor Corp.’s Chinese joint ventures are recalling more than 560,000 sport utility vehicles to fix a defect in the second-row seats that could jeopardize passenger safety during a collision. From April 1, the Japanese carmaker and its local partner …

item.title: Demand, Risks for Global Data Center Insurance Growing: Report
item.description: Fast growth in data center construction is pushing up insurance demand and risks for the sector, a report published by Swiss on Friday said. Global insurance premiums linked to data centers are expected to rise to $24.2 billion by 2030 from an estimated $10.6 billion currently, the report said. Noting that building costs for a … Continued

item.title: Roadless Rule Recision on Forest Lands Could Increase Wildfire Risk: Study
item.description: Department of Agriculture (USDA) announced that it was rescinding the 2001 Roadless Rule, but a study that followed indicated that by overturning the rule, wildfires were much more likely in areas near forest roads. The government agency said the rule was outdated and that rescinding it would remove prohibitions on road … Continued

item.title: Investment trust votes, conflicts of interest, and our role
item.description: On 3 March 2026, we said we’d bring forward our planned review of the UK Listing Rules for Investment entities, including how they apply to board independence and related party provisions.Since then, there has been substantial debate over our role in relation to investment trusts, including calls for us to ‘get to grips’ with voting rules ‘that allow a minority shareholder to repeatedly attack an investment trust’.Much of this debate suggests there are misunderstandings about how investment trusts are governed and where responsibilities sit. We’re concerned this may confuse investors in these trusts. Other calls to action have lacked clear proposals or been based on future hypothetical scenarios for which protections often exist.

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: A Varied Week For Big Deals, Led By AI And Defense
item.description: The pace of large-scale dealmaking picked up some this week, led by OpenAI’s disclosure that it raised another $10 billion. Other big financings went to startups and growth-stage companies in sectors including defense tech, enterprise AI, autonomy and even laundry.

item.title: Aon adds US SCS to its Automated Event Response service
item.description: This content is copyright to www.artemis.bm and should not appear anywhere else, or an infringement has occurred. Aon, the global insurance and reinsurance broking group, has expanded its Automated Event Response (AER) service to include US severe convective storms (SCS), further assisting carriers to manage losses from what Aon describes as the costliest insured peril of the 21st century on a cumulative basis. In January, Aon reported that global insured losses from […] Aon adds US SCS to its Automated Event Response service was published by: www.Artemis.bm Our catastrophe bond deal directory Sign up for our free weekly email newsletter here .

item.title: PERILS estimates €586m insured market loss from storm Nils
item.description: This content is copyright to www.artemis.bm and should not appear anywhere else, or an infringement has occurred. Extratropical windstorm Nils, also known as Ulrike, battered the Nouvelle-Aquitaine and Occitanie regions in southwest France in mid-February, causing insurance industry losses of €586 million, according to an initial estimate from catastrophe data aggregator, PERILS. Between February 11th and 13th, windstorm Nils brought damaging winds and intense rainfall, which impacted already saturated grounds after the […] PERILS estimates €586m insured market loss from storm Nils was published by: www.Artemis.bm Our catastrophe bond deal directory Sign up for our free weekly email newsletter here .

item.title: SEADRIF and AHA Centre sign agreement to advance disaster risk financing in Southeast Asia
item.description: The Southeast Asia Disaster Risk Insurance Facility (SEADRIF) Insurance Company, a regional initiative supporting disaster risk financing solutions, and the ASEAN Coordinating Centre for Humanitarian Assistance on disaster management (AHA Centre), the regional body for disaster response coordination, has recently entered into a Memorandum of Intent (MoI) in Bali, Indonesia. The agreement establishes a framework […] The post SEADRIF and AHA Centre sign agreement to advance disaster risk financing in Southeast Asia appeared first on ReinsuranceNe.ws .

item.title: XS Global appoints Daniel Abramson as Head of Construction Underwriting, APAC
item.description: XS Global, an independently owned managing general underwriting (MGU) platform, has announced the appointment of Daniel Abramson as Head of Construction Underwriting for Asia Pacific, based in Hong Kong. Abramson brings more than 25 years of insurance industry experience spanning construction and energy underwriting. Before joining XS Global, he was Head of Construction at Aviva.

item.title: Swiss Re flags accumulation risk in data centres as key underwriting challenge
item.description: The data centre industry is evolving into increasingly complex, high-energy-density facilities that concentrate risks within single sites, with underwriting success depending not only on capacity, but also on specialised technical assessment and disciplined accumulation management, according to a recent report by the Swiss Re Institute. Swiss Re highlighted that data centres powering today’s AI infrastructure […] The post Swiss Re flags accumulation risk in data centres as key underwriting challenge appeared first on ReinsuranceNe.ws .

item.title: People Moves: Marsh Risk Names Zafiriadis to Lead New Service Delivery Practice
item.description: Marsh names Zafiriadis Leader of new Service Delivery Practice, US and Canada, Marsh Risk Marsh, headquartered in New York City, appointed Katrina Zafiriadis as leader of its newly established service delivery practice (SDP), U.S.

item.title: Amid Drought, Corpus Christi Seeks Water From Privately Owned Plant
item.description: Six months after scrapping their own seawater desalination plant project, Corpus Christi City Council voted Tuesday to consider an agreement with a private company to purchase water from its desalination plant to help stave off an impending water emergency.

item.title: Ohio Contractor Sentenced to 17 Years in Prison on Fraud Charges
item.description: A pole barn contractor who defrauded consumers of more than $400,000 has been sentenced to at least 17 years in prison after being convicted of 47 felony counts, Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost announced this week..

item.title: Infosys to acquire Stratus to accelerate AI-led insurance transformation
item.description: Infosys has announced plans to acquire Stratus, a specialist technology solutions firm serving the property and casualty (P&C) insurance sector, in a move aimed at strengthening its capabilities in AI-driven digital transformation. The post Infosys to acquire Stratus to accelerate AI-led insurance transformation appeared first on Insurtech Insights .

item.title: HawkSoft and Gaya Announce Partnership to Automate Data Entry
item.description: Agencies can send customer and policy data from HawkSoft to Gaya to automate quoting workflows across carrier portals, raters, and insurance forms. HawkSoft and Gaya today announced a new integration... The post HawkSoft and Gaya Announce Partnership to Automate Data Entry appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights .

item.title: Safety System Failed to Prevent Deadly Runway Collision at LaGuardia Airport
item.description: The systems in place at New York’s LaGuardia Airport to prevent ground collisions failed to keep an Air Canada jet from smashing into a fire truck that had just pulled out on the runway as the plane was landing. The National Transportation Safety Board will determine what went wrong before Sunday’s crash that killed both … Continued

item.title: Peak Re, Suncorp, Allianz Jio Re, Aon, Lockton: 15 APAC insurance people moves of the week
item.description: Sompo, Pacific Insurance, Berkley Insurance Asia, XS Global, Markel, Marsh, Willis, Howden, Ando, Talbot, Tower, Niyam Group and PICC also made personnel changes over the last week. The post Peak Re, Suncorp, Allianz Jio Re, Aon, Lockton: 15 APAC insurance people moves of the week appeared first on InsuranceAsia News .

item.title: My FCA marks first year with a single, streamlined sign in for all firms
item.description: As part of ongoing improvements to My FCA, and following the successful removal of RegData sign in at the end of last year, we have now removed direct access to Connect and the Online Invoicing System. All existing RegData, Connect and Online Invoicing links and bookmarked pages will now automatically redirect to My FCA, where you can access all systems from a single homepage without signing in again. This makes managing your regulatory tasks quicker and more efficient.One year of My FCAOne year on from launch, My FCA has proven to be a real success.

item.title: FCA fines Dinosaur Merchant Bank Limited for market abuse surveillance failures
item.description: The FCA has fined Dinosaur Merchant Bank Limited (DMBL) £338,000 for failing to put in place effective systems and controls to detect and report suspicious trading in its contracts for difference (CFD) business. CFDs are sophisticated financial products that are used to speculate on various assets going up or down in value. Given their high-risk nature, firms must have strong and reliable surveillance arrangements to prevent insider dealing and market manipulation.In June 2024, DMBL introduced a new order system that led to a sharp increase in CFD trading by its clients.

item.title: Austin’s Star Is Still Shining Bright: Venture Funding To City’s Startups Hits All-Time High
item.description: Austin’s startup scene is stronger than ever. That’s the consensus among investors in the city, and it’s borne out by Crunchbase data, which shows startups headquartered in the Lone Star State’s capital raised a record $7.19 billion in venture funding in 2025.

item.title: GAM: Swiss Re ILS partnership enhanced liquidity, improved dealing terms. New products planned
item.description: This content is copyright to www.artemis.bm and should not appear anywhere else, or an infringement has occurred. GAM Investments has said that its catastrophe bond and insurance-linked securities partnership with global reinsurance giant Swiss Re has introduced enhanced liquidity and improved dealing terms across strategies and assets managed, while also saying new ILS product launches are planned for 2026. In 2025, the Swiss Re Insurance-Linked Investment Advisors Corporation (SRILIAC) registered investment adviser […] GAM: Swiss Re ILS partnership enhanced liquidity, improved dealing terms.
